name: Friends of KDSP
mission: Friends of KDSP supports the empowerment of the Down syndrome community in Pakistan by providing quality developmental services to individuals with Down syndrome and promoting and advocating for their inclusion in mainstream society.
theoryOfChange: Providing a one-stop solution of high-quality, subsidized developmental and healthcare services to enable individuals with Down syndrome to become independent and contributing members of society.
zakatClaimEvidence: On the Friends of KDSP donation page, under 'Select a campaign', 'Zakat' is explicitly listed as an option. Additionally, a YouTube video titled 'Transform lives, with your Zakat with Friends of KDSP' encourages zakat donations to the organization. The KDSP donation page, which directs US donors to Friends of KDSP, also explicitly states 'We accept Zakat' and provides 'Zakat' as a donation type option. (Source: https://friendsofkdsp.org)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 28 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 29/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

risks.description: Zakat-collecting organization holds 28 months of reserves — zakat should be distributed promptly

score_details.score_summary: Friends of Kdsp shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied). Evidence score may reflect limited reporting capacity, not program weakness.

zakat.claim_evidence: On the Friends of KDSP donation page, under 'Select a campaign', 'Zakat' is explicitly listed as an option. Additionally, a YouTube video titled 'Transform lives, with your Zakat with Friends of KDSP' encourages zakat donations to the organization. The KDSP donation page, which directs US donors to Friends of KDSP, also explicitly states 'We accept Zakat' and provides 'Zakat' as a donation type option. (Source: https://friendsofkdsp.org)

all_citations.claim: The charity reported total revenue of $848,904, a program expense ratio of 60.4%, and working capital of 28.5 months.
The organization has a fundraising efficiency of $0.23 per $1 raised.
Programs include Sehat Healthcare and Early Childhood Intervention, focusing on therapy sessions and school integration.
The charity is Zakat-eligible and provides options for religious giving.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The charity earns a moderate score reflecting its strong financial liquidity and clear mission, balanced against a lower program expense ratio and limited external data availability.
areas_for_improvement: program expense ratio of 60.4% suggests room to increase the proportion of spending on direct services [1]
Limited third-party data verification contributes to a low data confidence rating [2]

dimension_explanations.alignment: The charity shows medium alignment for Muslim donors due to its Zakat-eligible status and focus on global education and disability support [4]. The cause is considered urgent within the context of international development, though the track record is still being established in major databases [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building with a cost per beneficiary of approximately $123.88, though its program expense ratio is 60.4% [1][3]. While it successfully schedules weekly therapy sessions, the overall impact score is tempered by low data confidence and the need for more comprehensive outcome reporting [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Friends of Kdsp empowers the Down syndrome community in Pakistan through specialized healthcare, early intervention, and educational integration.
strengths: Maintains a robust working capital of 28.5 months, ensuring long-term operational stability [1]
Demonstrates high fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.23 per $1 raised [2]
Provides direct impact through weekly therapy sessions and mainstream school integration [3]
baseline_narrative.summary: Friends of Kdsp is a non-profit organization dedicated to supporting individuals with Down syndrome in Pakistan by providing essential services such as Sehat Healthcare and Early Childhood Intervention [1][3]. The organization is Zakat-eligible, offering donors a religiously compliant pathway to support disability advocacy and capacity-building programs [4].
